metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students explore photographic practice through study of camera fundamentals, composition principles, lighting techniques, and digital editing workflows. This course emphasizes photographic storytelling, aesthetic development, and the technical skills required to create compelling images. Students work with both traditional and digital photography approaches, developing their artistic vision and critical understanding of photography as contemporary art.
metadata.general_description: Create and analyze photographs using technical skills, composition principles, and digital editing to develop artistic vision.
cross_curricular_connections: English Language Arts (visual narrative and storytelling)

big_ideas.statement: Photography is a medium of creative vision that captures, interprets, and communicates moments through intentional technical and aesthetic choices.
big_ideas.elaboration: Photographers make deliberate decisions about framing, exposure, focus, and timing to create images that express their artistic vision and communicate meaning to viewers.

big_ideas.statement: Understanding camera mechanics and light principles enables photographers to realize their creative intent.
big_ideas.elaboration: Technical knowledge of exposure, aperture, shutter speed, focus, and lighting provides photographers with tools to control how images appear and communicate ideas.

big_ideas.statement: Composition and visual design principles guide the effective organization of photographic images.
big_ideas.elaboration: Strategic use of framing, perspective, depth, colour, and visual elements directs viewer attention and creates compelling visual narratives.

big_ideas.statement: Post-processing and digital editing are integral aspects of contemporary photographic practice.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital tools enable photographers to refine technical aspects, enhance visual impact, and realize their artistic vision through thoughtful editing practices.

competencies.raw: Master camera fundamentals and exposure control
i_can_statements: I can operate a camera and adjust exposure, aperture, shutter speed, and ISO appropriately
I can understand how aperture affects depth of field and image appearance
I can use shutter speed creatively to control motion and freeze action
I can manage exposure in various lighting conditions to achieve desired results

competencies.raw: Apply composition and design principles to create compelling photographs
i_can_statements: I can use rule of thirds, leading lines, and framing to guide viewer attention
I can create depth and perspective through intentional compositional choices
I can use negative space, balance, and visual weight effectively in compositions
I can experiment with different perspectives and camera angles to create varied viewpoints

competencies.raw: Work effectively with light and develop sophisticated lighting approaches
i_can_statements: I can identify and use natural light effectively in photography
I can manipulate and control artificial light to support visual storytelling
I can understand how colour, direction, and quality of light affect photographs
I can create compelling images in challenging lighting conditions

competencies.raw: Use digital editing tools to refine and enhance photographs
i_can_statements: I can use photo editing software to adjust exposure, colour, and contrast
I can apply selective editing and masks to enhance specific image areas
I can retouch and refine photographs while maintaining authenticity
I can experiment with editing techniques to achieve artistic effects

competencies.raw: Analyze photographs and understand how technical and aesthetic choices create meaning
i_can_statements: I can identify technical choices in photographs and explain their effects
I can analyze compositional strategies and their impact on viewer experience
I can recognize how colour, light, and visual elements communicate meaning
I can articulate the relationship between photographic choices and intended message

competencies.raw: Evaluate my own photography and identify areas for continued development
i_can_statements: I can assess technical execution, composition, and artistic effectiveness in my photographs
I can identify strengths in my photography and areas for improvement
I can recognize my developing eye and progress as a photographer
I can set meaningful goals for technical and artistic growth

competencies.raw: Understand photography as a medium for personal expression and storytelling
i_can_statements: I can explore how photography reflects personal perspective and experiences
I can recognize photography's potential to document, interpret, and communicate narratives
I can understand how photographers use their work to express social or cultural messages
I can reflect on my own artistic voice and what I want my photographs to communicate

competencies.raw: Develop critical perspective on photography in contemporary culture
i_can_statements: I can recognize how photography is used in media, advertising, and social contexts
I can understand issues of authenticity, manipulation, and representation in photography
I can appreciate diverse photographic traditions and contemporary photographic practices
I can reflect on photography's power and responsibility in shaping cultural understanding

competencies.raw: Create photographs that effectively communicate intended messages and artistic vision
i_can_statements: I can photograph subjects in ways that express my artistic perspective
I can execute technical and compositional planning to achieve desired photographic results
I can create cohesive photographic series that explore themes or concepts
I can present photographs that engage viewers with compelling visual narratives

competencies.raw: Share photographic work through presentations and exhibitions
i_can_statements: I can present photographs in classroom contexts or informal exhibitions
I can articulate the inspiration, process, and meaning behind my photographic work
I can organize photographs effectively for presentation or portfolio purposes
I can speak knowledgeably about my photographic choices and artistic intent

competencies.raw: Document photographic learning and artistic development
i_can_statements: I can maintain organized digital files and metadata for photographic work
I can write reflectively about photographic choices, process, and learning
I can analyze photographic work through written or verbal critique
I can track technical and artistic growth over time through documentation

competencies.raw: Engage in critique and feedback processes to strengthen photographic practice
i_can_statements: I can accept and incorporate constructive feedback on my photographic work
I can provide thoughtful critique to peers about their photographs
I can engage in meaningful discussions about photographic choices and meanings
I can use critique to refine my work and develop as a photographer

competencies.raw: Connect photography to other art forms and disciplines
i_can_statements: I can explore how photography relates to visual art, film, and other media
I can understand photography's role in documenting history and social issues
I can create multimedia projects that integrate photography with other artistic elements
I can recognize how photographic techniques apply across different disciplines

competencies.raw: Study diverse photographers and photographic traditions
i_can_statements: I can analyze and learn from the work of influential photographers across history
I can explore photographic traditions and approaches from diverse cultures
I can identify distinctive styles and innovative approaches in contemporary photography
I can understand how different photographers develop and evolve their artistic practice

competencies.raw: Explore photography as a career or ongoing artistic practice
i_can_statements: I can research career opportunities in photography, including commercial and fine art contexts
I can understand business and technical aspects of professional photography
I can develop a portfolio that demonstrates my photographic capabilities and vision
I can engage with mentorship and professional development opportunities in photography

competencies.raw: Build understanding of photography's impact on culture and society
i_can_statements: I can recognize photography's power in journalism, documentary, and social activism
I can understand ethical considerations in photographic practice and representation
I can appreciate photography's role in preserving cultural memory and identity
I can reflect on my own responsibility as a photographer in representing the world

areas.name: Camera Fundamentals and Exposure Control
topics: Camera types and mechanisms: DSLR, mirrorless, and traditional cameras
Exposure triangle: aperture, shutter speed, and ISO interactions
Depth of field and aperture effects on image appearance
Shutter speed and motion: freezing action and motion blur
Metering modes and exposure compensation techniques
Focus systems and autofocus versus manual focus

areas.name: Composition and Visual Design
topics: Rule of thirds, grid systems, and compositional frameworks
Leading lines, framing, and directing viewer attention
Depth, perspective, and spatial relationships in photographs
Colour theory and colour relationships in composition
Balance, symmetry, and visual weight in photographic design
Perspective, viewpoint, and camera angles as compositional tools

areas.name: Lighting Techniques and Light Management
topics: Natural light: quality, direction, and time of day effects
Artificial lighting: equipment, positioning, and control
Studio lighting setup and techniques for portraiture and product photography
Light ratios, fill light, and key light concepts
Colour temperature and white balance in various lighting contexts
Creative lighting techniques for mood and atmosphere

areas.name: Digital Editing and Photographic Storytelling
topics: Digital workflow: importing, organizing, and managing image files
Editing fundamentals: exposure, colour, contrast, and tone adjustments
Selective editing and masking techniques
Retouching and enhancing photographs
Series development and thematic storytelling through photography
Photography as documentation, interpretation, and artistic expression
